title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
pitch: A daydreamer finally lives out his fantasies by embarking on a real adventure. Gorgeous cinematography, zero cynicism.

title: The Menu
pitch: A celebrity chef serves a terrifying multi-course meal to wealthy guests on a private island. Darkly hilarious.

title: Challengers
pitch: A love triangle between tennis players told backwards through a championship match. Luca Guadagnino at his most electric.

title: The Holdovers
pitch: A grumpy classics teacher and a troubled student are stuck at boarding school over Christmas 1970. Quietly devastating.

title: Borat
pitch: A Kazakh journalist travels across America. What he exposes about his hosts is funnier than anything he does.

title: Hereditary
pitch: A family unravels after their grandmother's death. The less you know going in, the harder it hits.

title: Chef
pitch: A top chef quits his job and starts a food truck with his son. Essentially a warm hug in movie form.

title: Poor Things
pitch: A reanimated woman discovers the world with the curiosity of a child and the freedom of someone with nothing to lose.

title: Tucker and Dale vs Evil
pitch: Two hillbillies are mistaken for murderers by clueless college kids. Every horror trope flipped on its head.

title: Uncut Gems
pitch: Adam Sandler as a gambling-addicted jeweler in a two-hour anxiety attack. You won't breathe for the last 30 minutes.

title: Puss in Boots: The Last Wish
pitch: A swashbuckling cat confronts mortality. Way better than it has any right to be — the animation style is breathtaking.

title: The Zone of Interest
pitch: The commandant of Auschwitz and his family live an idyllic life next door to the camp. Horror through absence.

title: Step Brothers
pitch: Two middle-aged men become stepbrothers and act like children. Stupid in the most brilliant way possible.

title: Killers of the Flower Moon
pitch: The systematic murder of Osage Nation members for oil money in 1920s Oklahoma. Scorsese, DiCaprio, De Niro. A slow burn that sears.

title: Spirited Away
pitch: A girl enters a world of spirits and must work in a bathhouse to save her parents. Miyazaki's masterpiece.

title: Anatomy of a Fall
pitch: A woman is accused of killing her husband. The trial dissects their marriage. You'll argue about the verdict for days.

title: The Hangover
pitch: Three groomsmen wake up in Vegas with no memory, a tiger, and a missing groom. The blueprint for modern comedy.

title: Dunkirk
pitch: The evacuation of Dunkirk told from land, sea, and air simultaneously. Almost no dialogue, all tension.

title: Fantastic Mr. Fox
pitch: A fox can't resist one last heist against three nasty farmers. Wes Anderson in stop-motion. Pure charm.

title: Her
pitch: A lonely man falls in love with his AI assistant. Sounds ridiculous. Feels painfully real.

title: Bridesmaids
pitch: A maid of honour slowly loses her mind planning a wedding. Kristen Wiig is a force of nature.

title: Saltburn
pitch: An Oxford student insinuates himself into an aristocratic family's country estate. Seductive and deeply unsettling.

title: Studio Ghibli: My Neighbor Totoro
pitch: Two sisters discover friendly forest spirits in rural Japan. No villain, no conflict, just warmth and wonder.

title: 12 Angry Men
pitch: Twelve jurors deliberate a murder verdict in a single room. Made in 1957, still the tensest courtroom drama ever.

title: The Death of Stalin
pitch: Soviet leaders scramble for power after Stalin dies. A farce played completely straight. Armando Iannucci's sharpest work.

title: Oppenheimer
pitch: The man who built the bomb and the world that judged him for it. Three hours that feel like a detonation.

title: Little Miss Sunshine
pitch: A dysfunctional family road-trips to a children's beauty pageant. The van scene. The dance scene. Perfection.

title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
pitch: A man erases memories of his ex-girlfriend and then fights to keep them. The most honest love story ever made.

title: Palm Springs
pitch: Two wedding guests get stuck in a time loop. Smarter than Groundhog Day, funnier than it should be.

title: The Revenant
pitch: A frontiersman is left for dead after a bear attack and crawls through the wilderness for revenge. Raw survival cinema.

title: Howl's Moving Castle
pitch: A girl cursed with old age finds refuge in a wizard's walking castle. Miyazaki at his most romantic.

title: The Master
pitch: A WWII veteran falls under the spell of a charismatic cult leader. Hoffman and Phoenix in a psychological cage match.

title: Eurovision Song Contest: The Story of Fire Saga
pitch: An Icelandic duo enters Eurovision. Will Ferrell sings with passion. The song-along scene is genuine magic.

title: Gone Girl
pitch: A wife disappears. The husband looks guilty. Then the story flips inside out. Fincher and Rosamund Pike at their coldest.

title: The Mitchells vs. the Machines
pitch: A dysfunctional family accidentally becomes humanity's last hope against a robot apocalypse. Frenetic, heartfelt, hilarious.

title: Minari
pitch: A Korean-American family starts a farm in Arkansas. Quiet, deeply felt, and the grandmother steals every scene.

title: Spy
pitch: A CIA desk analyst goes undercover as a spy. Melissa McCarthy and Jason Statham are comedy gold together.

title: There Will Be Blood
pitch: An oil prospector's ambition consumes everything. Daniel Day-Lewis gives the most terrifying performance of all time.

title: The Darjeeling Limited
pitch: Three brothers take a train across India to find their mother. Peak Wes Anderson melancholy disguised as a travelogue.

title: Being John Malkovich
pitch: A puppeteer discovers a portal into John Malkovich's brain. Charlie Kaufman's brain is weirder than any sci-fi.

title: Tropic Thunder
pitch: Actors filming a war movie accidentally stumble into a real conflict. Tom Cruise's cameo alone is worth it.

title: Annihilation
pitch: Five scientists enter a mutating zone where the laws of nature break down. The lighthouse scene will haunt you.

title: Kiki's Delivery Service
pitch: A young witch moves to a new city and starts a delivery service on her broomstick. Pure Ghibli comfort.

title: American Fiction
pitch: A Black novelist writes a satirical book playing into stereotypes — and it becomes a bestseller. Razor-sharp on race and publishing.

title: The Grand Escape
pitch: Allied POWs plan a massive escape from a German camp. Steve McQueen on a motorcycle. Old-school adventure at its finest.

title: The Gentlemen
pitch: A British drug lord tries to sell his empire. Guy Ritchie at his sharpest, Hugh Grant at his slimiest.

title: Blade Runner 2049
pitch: A replicant cop uncovers a secret that could shatter society. Somehow a worthy sequel to a masterpiece.

title: The Other Guys
pitch: Two mismatched NYPD detectives accidentally uncover a massive financial crime. Will Ferrell and Mark Wahlberg are a perfect pair.

title: Nope
pitch: Siblings at a horse ranch discover something terrifying in the sky. Jordan Peele's most ambitious and spectacular film.

title: Up
pitch: An old man ties thousands of balloons to his house and flies to South America. The first 10 minutes will wreck you.

title: Triangle of Sadness
pitch: Influencers and billionaires on a luxury yacht. Then the yacht sinks. Class dynamics invert violently. The dinner scene is legendary.

title: Shaun of the Dead
pitch: A slacker tries to get his life together during a zombie apocalypse. The first and best of the Cornetto trilogy.

title: The Departed
pitch: A cop infiltrates the mob while a mobster infiltrates the police. Nobody knows who's who. Scorsese finally gets his Oscar.

title: WALL·E
pitch: A lonely robot on an abandoned Earth falls in love with a sleek probe. Almost no dialogue. All heart.

title: Call Me by Your Name
pitch: A summer romance in 1980s Italy between a teenager and his father's research assistant. The final monologue by Michael Stuhlbarg is everything.

title: Hot Fuzz
pitch: A supercop is reassigned to a sleepy village where nothing happens. Then people start dying. Edgar Wright's tightest comedy.

title: Oldboy
pitch: A man is imprisoned for 15 years with no explanation, then released. The reason why is the most shocking twist in cinema.

title: Encanto
pitch: A Colombian family all have magical powers — except one. The music is impossibly catchy and the family dynamics feel real.

title: Inside Out 2
pitch: Anxiety shows up in Riley's teenage brain and takes over. Pixar makes you cry about emotions having emotions. Again.

title: 21 Jump Street
pitch: Two cops go undercover at a high school and discover they're no longer cool. Channing Tatum's comedy awakening.

title: Apocalypto
pitch: A Mayan villager is captured for sacrifice and runs for his life through the jungle. Relentless, visceral, entirely in Yucatec Maya.

title: Lost in Translation
pitch: Two Americans adrift in Tokyo form an unlikely bond. Sofia Coppola captures loneliness without a single dramatic beat.

title: Conclave
pitch: Cardinals gather in the Vatican to elect a new pope. Political intrigue, secrets, and a final twist that reframes everything.

title: This Is Spinal Tap
pitch: A documentary crew follows Britain's loudest band on their disastrous American tour. These go to eleven.

title: Memoria
pitch: A woman in Colombia hears a mysterious bang that nobody else can hear. Apichatpong Weerasethakul's most hypnotic film.

title: Se7en
pitch: Two detectives hunt a serial killer using the seven deadly sins. The final scene is cinema's cruelest gut punch.

title: About Time
pitch: A man discovers he can travel back in time. He uses it to find love. The real story is about his dad.

title: The Florida Project
pitch: A six-year-old has a magical summer in a motel near Disney World. The gap between her joy and her reality is heartbreaking.

title: Airplane!
pitch: A parody of disaster films with more jokes per minute than any movie before or since. Don't call me Shirley.

title: The Batman
pitch: Batman as a detective noir. Gotham has never looked this rain-soaked or felt this corrupt. Pattinson nails the damaged vigilante.

title: Porco Rosso
pitch: A WWI flying ace has been cursed into a pig and now hunts air pirates over the Adriatic. Peak Ghibli vibes.

title: The Substance
pitch: An aging celebrity uses a black-market drug that creates a younger version of herself. Body horror meets Hollywood satire.

title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail
pitch: King Arthur and his knights quest for the Holy Grail. The Black Knight. The French taunters. The killer rabbit. Timeless.

title: Manchester by the Sea
pitch: A man returns to his hometown to care for his nephew after tragedy. Casey Affleck carries the weight of the world.

title: School of Rock
pitch: A failing musician poses as a substitute teacher and turns his class into a rock band. Jack Black's most lovable performance.

title: Anora
pitch: A Brooklyn stripper marries the son of a Russian oligarch. Then his parents find out. Palme d'Or winner for a reason.

title: Burn After Reading
pitch: Gym employees find a CIA agent's memoir and try to blackmail him. Everyone is an idiot. The Coens' most cynical farce.

title: Tenet
pitch: Time moves forwards and backwards simultaneously. You won't fully understand it. You'll want to watch it again immediately.

title: Studio Ghibli: The Wind Rises
pitch: The life of the engineer who designed Japan's WWII fighter planes. Miyazaki's most personal and morally complex film.

title: Mid90s
pitch: A 13-year-old finds belonging with a group of skaters in 90s LA. Jonah Hill's directorial debut, shot on 16mm. Nostalgic and tender.

title: Napoleon Dynamite
pitch: An awkward Idaho teenager helps his friend run for class president. The dance scene is cinema's greatest underdog moment.

title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
pitch: Nic Cage in a leather suit rigged with explosives in a post-apocalyptic Japan. It's exactly as unhinged as it sounds.

title: Sing Street
pitch: A Dublin teenager starts a band to impress a girl in 1980s Ireland. The songs are genuinely great. John Carney's best.

title: Never Let Me Go
pitch: Students at an idyllic English boarding school discover the horrifying purpose they were created for. Quiet devastation.

title: Deadpool
pitch: A mercenary with accelerated healing becomes the world's most irritating superhero. Breaks the fourth wall and every R-rated boundary.

title: The Lighthouse
pitch: Two lighthouse keepers go mad on a remote island. Shot in black and white, square format. Dafoe and Pattinson screaming into the void.

title: Moonrise Kingdom
pitch: Two 12-year-olds run away together on a New England island. Wes Anderson's most tender film, somehow.

title: The Lives of Others
pitch: A Stasi agent in East Berlin begins to sympathize with the couple he's spying on. The final line will stay with you forever.

title: Bottoms
pitch: Two unpopular girls start a fight club to hook up with cheerleaders. Absurdist high school comedy that goes completely off the rails.

title: Civil War
pitch: War journalists cross a fractured America during a second civil war. Alex Garland makes you feel every bullet and moral compromise.

title: Whisper of the Heart
pitch: A bookish girl follows the trail of a mysterious boy through Tokyo's streets. Ghibli's most grounded and romantic coming-of-age.

title: Shoplifters
pitch: A family of petty thieves in Tokyo takes in an abused child. Hirokazu Kore-eda asks what makes a family real.

title: Dodgeball
pitch: A ragtag gym enters a dodgeball tournament to save their business. Ben Stiller's villain is peak absurdity.

title: The Raid
pitch: A SWAT team is trapped in a building full of killers and must fight their way up. The most intense action film ever made. Indonesian.

title: The Princess Bride
pitch: A fairy tale with fencing, giants, true love, and Inigo Montoya. Inconceivable that you haven't seen it.

title: I'm Still Here
pitch: A Brazilian woman's husband is taken by the military dictatorship in the 1970s. She refuses to be silent. Fernanda Torres is extraordinary.

title: Snatch
pitch: A stolen diamond, bare-knuckle boxing, and Brad Pitt speaking incomprehensible Irish. Guy Ritchie's tightest, funniest script.

title: Under the Skin
pitch: Scarlett Johansson drives around Scotland picking up men. She's not human. Nothing will prepare you for this film.

title: The Boy and the Heron
pitch: A grieving boy follows a mysterious heron into a fantastical world. Miyazaki's farewell film — strange, beautiful, deeply personal.

title: A Separation
pitch: An Iranian couple's divorce unravels into a moral crisis where everyone is right and everyone is wrong. Farhadi's masterpiece.
